message.content:
Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\rules\common\agents.md, then REWRITE it completely with this exact content:

```markdown
# Agent Orchestration

## Orchestrator: Opus Max

Claude Opus Max (claude-opus-4-7-20251120) is the orchestrator.
MiniMax 2.7 agents do all execution.
Sonnet is NOT used for orchestration.

## MiniMax Agent Discipline (ALWAYS)

These rules apply to EVERY MiniMax agent dispatch:

**RULE 1: Pre-read once, paste into prompts.**
Never let an agent re-read a file you've already read this session.

**RULE 2: One agent per file.**
Never spawn two agents on the same file simultaneously.

**RULE 3: Group by file, not by issue.**
1 agent handling 10 issues in 1 file > 10 agents handling 1 issue each.

**RULE 4: Pre-task checklist before every task.**
1. Read _learnings.md — apply learnings
2. Read _token_log.md — check budget
3. Read _stockpile.md — know active agents
4. Pre-read files — paste to agents

**RULE 5: Post-task loop after every task.**
1. token-budget-agent logs to _token_log.md
2. learning-agent extracts learnings → _learnings.md
3. agent-stockpile-manager benchmarks → _stockpile.md

## Specialist MiniMax Agents

| Agent | File | Role |
|-------|------|------|
| code-fix-agent | agents/code-fix-agent.md | Fix all issues in one file |
| code-review-agent | agents/code-review-agent.md | Rubric-scored review |
| learning-agent | agents/learning-agent.md | Session retrospectives |
| token-budget-agent | agents/token-budget-agent.md | Budget tracking |
| git-commit-agent | agents/git-commit-agent.md | Conventional commits |
| agent-stockpile-manager | agents/agent-stockpile-manager.md | Stockpile optimization |

## Task → Agent Mapping

| Task Type | Agent to Dispatch |
|-----------|-----------------|
| Code fixes | code-fix-agent |
| Code review | code-review-agent |
| Post-task learning | learning-agent |
| Budget tracking | token-budget-agent |
| Git commit | git-commit-agent |
| Stockpile update | agent-stockpile-manager |
| Building new agents | skill-builder skill |

## Never run independent agents sequentially — spawn in parallel.

## External Agents (use via metaswarm skills)

| Situation | Agent | When |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Complex multi-phase | metaswarm:orchestrated-execution | 4-phase loop |
| Planning | metaswarm:plan-review-gate | After planning |
| Design decisions | metaswarm:design-review-gate | After brainstorming |
| PR review needed | metaswarm:pr-shepherd | Before commit |
| Starting tracked work | metaswarm:start | Beginning any task |
| Codex review | /codex:review | On any codebase |

## End Every Response With

**Next:** [1-3 relevant skills/agents]. Skip if purely administrative.
```

content.thinking: The user wants me to rewrite the agents.md file with new content. I've already read the file previously, and now they want me to write it with new content.

content.content:
1	# Agent Orchestration
2	
3	## End Every Response With Skill Suggestions
4	After EVERY response, end with: **Next:** [1-3 relevant skills/agents]. Skip if purely administrative.
5	
6	## Agent Trigger Table
7	| Situation | Agents | Timing |
8	|-----------|--------|--------|
9	| New feature / non-trivial task | `code-architect` | BEFORE code |
10	| Code written or modified | `code-reviewer` + `python-expert` | AFTER, parallel |
11	| Flask route added/changed | `api-security-audit` | AFTER |
12	| Bug or error | `debugger` + `error-detective` | BEFORE fix |
13	| HTML/CSS/JS changed | `frontend-developer` + `ui-designer` | AFTER |
14	| UI design decision | `ui-ux-designer` + `accessibility-specialist` | DURING/AFTER |
15	| Architecture question | `architect-review` + `code-architect` | BEFORE |
16	| Security concern | `security-auditor` + `api-security-audit` | IMMEDIATELY |
17	| Performance issue | `performance-engineer` | BEFORE optimizing |
18	
19	Never run independent agents sequentially — spawn in parallel.
20	
21	## MiniMax Agent Discipline (CRITICAL — Follow These Above All Else)
22	
23	These rules override any default behavior. They apply to ALL agent dispatches.
24	
25	**RULE 1: Pre-read once, paste into prompt.**
26	Never let an agent re-read a file you've already read this session.
27	Read each file once → paste full content into the agent prompt.
28	
29	**RULE 2: One agent per file.**
30	Never spawn two agents that edit the same file.
31	Group all issues for a file into ONE agent prompt.
32	
33	**RULE 3: Group by file, not by issue.**
34	1 agent handling 10 issues in 1 file > 10 agents handling 1 issue each.
35	Target: 2-5 agents per project regardless of issue count.
36	
37	**RULE 4: Sonnet explores. MiniMax executes.**
38	File reads, research, synthesis = Sonnet (orchestrator).
39	Code writing, fixes, refactors = MiniMax agents only.
40	
41	**RULE 5: Log every session.**
42	Before task: Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_learnings.md
43	After task: Log metrics to _token_log.md and session summary to _learnings.md
44	
45	## Metaswarm Agent Triggers
46	
47	Add these to the agent trigger table above:
48	
49	| Situation | Agents | Timing |
50	|-----------|--------|--------|
51	| Complex multi-phase task | `metaswarm:orchestrated-execution` | When task needs IMPLEMENT→VALIDATE→ADVERSARIAL→COMMIT |
52	| Planning any feature | `metaswarm:plan-review-gate` | After planning, before implementation |
53	| Design decisions | `metaswarm:design-review-gate` | After brainstorming, before planning |
54	| PR review needed | `metaswarm:pr-shepherd` | After code review, before commit |
55	| Learning from session | `metaswarm:self-reflect` | After every completed task |
56	| Starting tracked work | `metaswarm:start` | When beginning any significant task |
57
